title: Max Heap Height Invariant Broken Due to Incorrect Heap Property Enforcement

statement.markdown: Implement a function `heapify` that takes a list of integers, `nums`, and builds a max heap from it. The function should use a bottom-up heapify approach and ensure the max heap property is maintained. The time complexity of the function should be O(n).
statement.inputFormat: A list of integers `nums` representing the input array.
statement.outputFormat: None. The function should modify the input array directly.
statement.notes: The max heap property states that for every node i, the value of the node should be greater than or equal to the values of its children. Use a bottom-up approach to build the max heap.

examples.explanation: The function should modify the input array to form a max heap.

hints.text: A max heap is a complete binary tree where each node is greater than or equal to its children.
To maintain the max heap property, ensure that the root of the tree is the largest element and that all children are less than their parent.
Use a bottom-up approach to heapify the array, starting from the last non-leaf node and moving up to the root.
At each step, compare the current node with its children and swap them if necessary to maintain the max heap property.

editorial.title: Max Heap Height Invariant Broken Due to Incorrect Heap Property Enforcement
editorial.intuition: Building a max heap from an unsorted array involves ensuring that the max heap property is maintained. This can be achieved using a bottom-up heapify approach.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach would involve creating a new max heap from the given array each time, resulting in O(n log n) time complexity.
editorial.betterSolution: Using a bottom-up heapify approach can reduce the time complexity to O(n log n) by reducing the number of operations required to maintain the max heap property.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a bottom-up heapify approach with a time complexity of O(n), ensuring that the max heap property is maintained while minimizing the number of operations.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by showing that the max heap property is maintained at each step of the heapify process.
editorial.dryRun: To perform a dry run, start with an unsorted array and visualize the heapify process, ensuring that the max heap property is maintained at each step.
commonMistakes: Failing to compare the current node with its children
Incorrectly swapping nodes to maintain the max heap property
